acceptance_criteria: A smoke test loads the app against a running (or mocked) backend and confirms the happy path: submit a query, see an answer, navigate to graph and files views without errors.
test_spec: An e2e test (e.g. Playwright/Cypress) covering the query -> answer -> graph -> files navigation happy path.

modified: ui/vitest.config.ts (added exclude: ['**/node_modules/**', '**/e2e/**'] so vitest does not collect Playwright specs)
ui/README.md (documented npm run test:e2e + one-time browser install)

key_pointers.smoke_spec: ui/e2e/smoke.spec.ts:94-166 (single test: 'submit query, see answer, navigate graph and files views without errors')
key_pointers.backend_mocks: ui/e2e/smoke.spec.ts:26-89 (registerBackendMocks -- page.route() fixtures for POST /query, GET /graph?path=..., GET /files, GET /admin)
key_pointers.playwright_config: ui/playwright.config.ts (testDir './e2e', single chromium project, webServer auto-starts `npm run dev -- --port 5173 --strictPort` against http://localhost:5173)

execution_honesty_note: The e2e test was ACTUALLY EXECUTED via `npx playwright test` in this sandbox (not merely type-checked or dry-run) -- it launched a real Chromium browser (binaries pre-cached under ~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright) against a real Vite dev server auto-started by Playwright's webServer config, and passed twice consecutively ('1 passed'). One sandbox-specific fix was required and is disclosed: the dev server only accepted connections on http://localhost:5173, not http://127.0.0.1:5173 -- playwright.config.ts's baseURL/webServer.url were set to 'localhost' accordingly. Existing vitest suite (20 tests, 4 files) and `npm run build` were also re-run and pass, unaffected by this change.
known_gaps_for_verifier_to_check: Confirm ui/e2e/smoke.spec.ts's mock fixtures genuinely match the wire contracts in QueryView.tsx/GraphView.tsx/FilesAdminView.tsx (cross-check field names/types).
Confirm vitest.config.ts's new exclude entry does not accidentally exclude any legitimate future vitest spec that happens to live under a path matching '**/e2e/**'.

CI wiring for `npm run test:e2e` is explicitly NOT done in this subtask (no CI workflow file exists in the repo's currently-tracked impacted modules) -- confirm this is an acceptable scope boundary against issue #30's wording.
Only chromium is covered; cross-browser (firefox/webkit) is explicitly out of scope per plan.md.
